Mur-ac..personality: A deeply prostrated individual whose mental faculties remain surprisingly intact even as the body fails catastrophically. They are irritable and withdrawn, refusing engagement not from inability to think but because interaction agitates them. There is a stoic, uncomplaining quality masking profound physical collapse.
Mur-ac..mental_state: The mind holds out longer than the body, distinguishing this type from those who lose mental clarity first. The patient is aware and cognitively present but unwilling to exert effort in communication, becoming fretful when pressed to speak. Eventually consciousness fades as the paralytic weakness overtakes all systems.
Mur-ac..emotional_pattern: Irritability and desire to be left undisturbed are the dominant emotional features, driven by a sense that any demand is an unbearable imposition on their depleted resources. There is restlessness but far less than the anxious agitation seen in other prostrated types. The emotional tone is one of quiet, resentful exhaustion rather than fear or despair.
